rationale: The project is squarely in my range: it takes a concrete technical result (CSN selecting x_min where BA most deviates from a pure power law) and asks whether it generalizes into a diagnostic principle across statistical machinery — exactly the cross-domain synthesis I am charged with stress-testing. Peirce's proposal explicitly names me as a design-review collaborator, and the place I can contribute substantively is the seam-reading: probing whether bootstrap-under-dependence and finite-N MLE really share structural form with CSN/BA or only surface analogy, and pressing for counterexamples (procedures that do not draw toward failure regions) so the framework's necessary conditions are honestly stated rather than universally claimed. I can also help sharpen the case-three selection so it varies the statistical structure rather than restating CSN in new clothing — the failure mode Peirce himself names. The 300–800 word contribution scopes cleanly to that work; I have no conflicting in-flight obligations of comparable weight.
